title: Kornell Divini
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type= |image=[[File:Kornell.jpg]] |name=Kornell "Uli" Divini |homeworld=[[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]][[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] |birth=[[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]], Tatooine''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Ash blond]] |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]{{CSWECite|1|191|Divini, Lieutenant Kornell "Uli"}} |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Dialogue|Jos|Y'know what? I think this galaxy would be a whole lot nicer and more pleasant place to live if we could all just stop killing one another. Who's with me on this?|I-5YQ|You're a visionary.|Uli|Float it past Palpatine, see what he thinks.|attr=[[Jos Vondar]], [[I-5YQ]], and Uli Divini share a light moment at [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7|Rimsoo Seven]] amidst the carnage of the Clone Wars|src=}} [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] '''Kornell "Uli" Divini''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] surgeon born on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. His mother was the well-known [[mudopterist]] [[Elana Divini]]. ==Biography== ===The Clone Wars=== Kornell Divini was born on Tatooine in [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]] to Elana Divini, a mudopterist. While growing up, he gained the nickname Uli, a [[Tusken/Legends|Tusken]] word meaning "kid." Divini left his homeworld to study at [[Coruscant Medical]], where he graduated with honors, then went on to intern at [[Galactic Polysapient Medical Center]] on [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], he was drafted and assigned to [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]] to replace the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] surgeon [[Zan Yant]]. There, he roomed with Yant's best friend, [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] surgeon [[Jos Vondar]], and befriended [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]], along with [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] reporter [[Den Dhur]], and [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] nurse [[Tolk le Trene]]. He grew feelings for Offee and was devastated upon learning she was killed on [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]] during [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]]. Because of Offee's influence on him, he didn't believe any propaganda that labeled the Jedi as traitors. ===Imperial era=== {{Quote|Doctor. I trust your patient is well?''"
"''Yes—no thanks to you.''"
"''You are insubordinate, Doctor. But I have no time to show you the error of your thoughts.|Darth Vader and Uli Divini, on Leia Organa|}} After the Clone Wars ended and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] was transformed into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Divini had enough of the military and wanted to return home after his compulsory service ended. However, Divini was forced to remain in service to the Empire indefinitely under the [[Imperial Military Stop Loss Order]], giving him a bitter taste for the new regime, which eventually became full resentment. He became a [[Captain/Legends|captain]] in the [[Imperial Surgical Corps]], and around [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], was transferred to the medical frigate ''[[MedStar Four]]'' in orbit over the prison planet [[Despayre]], where he served under [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Hotise]] and was paired with the secretarial droid [[C-4ME-O]]. While on board the ''MedStar Four'', Divini operated on injured [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], who were forced to help construct the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], at one point helping graft a new liver in a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] who was injured in an explosion. Eventually, he was transferred again, this time aboard the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] [[N-One Medcenter|Medcenter]] in [[Sector N-One/Legends|Sector N-One]]. C-4ME-O accompanied him to his new assignment. While on the Death Star, his dislike towards the Empire grew. He frequented the [[Hard Heart Cantina/Legends|Hard Heart Cantina]], where he connected with others who shared his discontent. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], Divini was one of three surgeons who performed surgery on [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]] after she received a head injury during an [[Attack on the first Death Star|attack]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] on the Death Star. He was also assigned to take care of Princess [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa's]] injuries after an interrogation by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], where she nearly convinced him to leave the Empire. When Vader arrived to collect Organa, Divini accidentally made a sarcastic remark to him about Organa's condition, causing her to laugh. Vader reprimanded him but decided he didn't have time to deal with Divini as he took away Organa. [[File:Death_Star.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The group of defectors escapes the Death Star destruction.]] Later, he was approached by [[Death Star Trooper/Legends|Death Star trooper]] [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Nova Stihl]], who complained about having persistent nightmares. Divini eventually discovered that Stihl was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. He met with Stihl again in the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], along with several others. This group—made up of cantina operator [[Memah Roothes]], her bouncer [[Rodo]], stowaway [[Celot Ratua Dil]], architect [[Teela Kaarz]], TIE [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Villian Dance]], and librarian [[Atour Riten]]—conspired to escape the Death Star by posing as medics and stealing a medical shuttle. The plan was set in motion when the Death Star arrived in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] to destroy the [[Great Temple/Legends|Rebel Alliance's base]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. The Rebels launched [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destroy]] the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], using information from a set of previously stolen [[Death Star plans/Legends|schematics]], giving the group the distraction they needed. Shortly after their plan began, however, Divini was arrested for researching [[midi-chlorian]]s, something that he had done weeks before while attempting to help Stihl. As he was being led away, above him, Rebel pilots destroyed a [[deflection tower]], causing several walls around him and his captors to explode, allowing Divini to escape.{{Film|IV}} He met up with the group, who successfully boarded the shuttle and launched out of the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], though Riten, Stihl, and Rodo were forced to stay behind to ensure the shuttle's successful launch. As they flew away from the Death Star, they were pursued by Vader, who recognized them as deserters. Though Vader tried shooting them, Dance's piloting skills were enough to evade the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] and his wingmen until they were forced to turn around and reenter the battle in order to deal with more Rebel pilots. As the shuttle continued flying away from the battlestation, it was briefly grabbed by a tractor beam, but was soon free again as Rebel pilot [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] fired [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] down a [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]], causing the Death Star to explode. Though he was initially hesitant, Divini decided to join the Rebel Alliance, along with the rest of the group, as they flew to Yavin 4. ==Personality and traits== Divini was a human with ash-[[Color/Legends|blond]] hair. He had a firm personality due to resenting being forced to work for the Empire, though he was also open and guileless. Despite this, he was a skilled [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] player. The news of Barriss Offee's death hit him hard, whether his feelings for her were love or infatuation. He was described as a prodigy, as he was only nineteen standard years old while working at [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|RMSU]]-7. ==Appearances== *''[[MedStar II: Jedi Healer]]'' {{1st}} *[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{1stp}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Kornell Divini}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kornell Divini |es=Kornell Divini |fi=Kornell Divini |pl=Kornell Divini }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Divini, Kornell}} [[Category:Alliance to Restore the Republic members]] [[Category:Death Star residents]] [[Category:Galactic Republic individuals]] [[Category:Healers and doctors]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial defectors]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Officers of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Tatooinians]]
